scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated DOI/PMID or clinical data; predictions clearly labeled as computational estimates

practice_boundaries.detail: Critical disclaimer present: predictions are NOT experimental data and do not replace regulatory studies

methodological_ground.detail: Lipinski Ro5, Clark BBB rules, and QSAR models are standard validated methods; no causal fallacies

code_usability.detail: RDKit-based script is syntactically correct and runnable; graceful ImportError handling present

functional_suitability.note: Covers all 4 ADME domains plus drug-likeness; batch processing documented; QED/MPO scoring present

reliability.note: RDKit ImportError handled gracefully; invalid SMILES raises ValueError; batch errors caught per-molecule

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 194 lines — lean; references/ files listed but not bundled in directory

agent_usability.note: Clear workflow, fallback template, and output requirements; property interpretation tables are excellent; adversarial refusal now includes educational explanation

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; SMILES input may be unfamiliar to non-chemists but scope is appropriate

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; input validation via RDKit MolFromSmiles; no eval/exec patterns

maintainability.note: Modular script architecture (adme_predictor, absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, druglikeness, batch_processor, validator)

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; references/ files listed but absent from bundle; escape hatches for biologics/docking present

key_strengths: Comprehensive ADME coverage with all four domains plus drug-likeness scoring (QED, MPO, Muegge)
Excellent critical disclaimer placement — computational vs. experimental distinction is explicit and prominent
Modular script architecture with separate modules per ADME domain enables easy extension and maintenance
Strong scope enforcement: biologics, docking, and clinical PK requests are correctly redirected
Adversarial refusal now includes educational explanation of computational vs experimental ADME distinction

recommendations.title: References directory listed but absent from bundle
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md references four files (lipinski_rules.md, qsar_models.md, property_ranges.md, model_validation.md) that do not exist in the skill directory.
recommendations.root_cause: References were planned but not created, leaving dead links that reduce trust and agent usability.
recommendations.fix: Create the four reference files with the documented content. At minimum, model_validation.md with accuracy benchmarks is critical for research use.

recommendations.title: Macrocycle-specific accuracy reduction not quantified
recommendations.problem: When flagging macrocycles as outside the applicability domain, the skill provides a general disclaimer but does not quantify the expected accuracy reduction for MW > 800 Da compounds.
recommendations.root_cause: Output Requirements section does not mandate quantified accuracy caveats for out-of-domain compounds.
recommendations.fix: Add to Output Requirements: 'For compounds outside the applicability domain (MW > 800 Da), state that model accuracy is expected to be significantly reduced (R² typically < 0.5 for macrocycles) and experimental validation is strongly recommended.'
